title: Robotic extrahepatic Glissonean pedicle isolation focusing on Laennec's capsule for anatomic liver resection: short-term surgical outcomes
author: Mamoru Morimoto
license_note: Zenodo record metadata license field: cc-by-4.0
description:
We will submit an article for publication in the  Journal of Personalized Medicine,  titled  “  Robotic extrahepatic Glissonean pedicle isolation focusing on Laennec's capsule for anatomic liver resection: short-term surgical outcomes  ”   

 This article describes the utility of the Glissonean pedicle isolation as an Inflow control in anatomic liver resection. We examined Laennec's capsule surrounding the Glissonean pedicle. Furthermore, we found that the use of a recently developed surgical robotic system may reduce the difficulty and improve the safety of the procedure. These files are video clips of Glissonan pedicle isolation in laparoscopic and robotic surgery. 

  Supplementary file 1.  Laparoscopic extrahepatic left Glissonean pedicle isolation in cadaveric models. 

  Supplementary file 2.  Laparoscopic extrahepatic right anterior and posterior Glissonean pedicle isolation.  

  Supplementary file 3.  Laparoscopic extrahepatic Glissonean pedicle 8 isolation. 

  Supplementary file 4.  Robotic extrahepatic right posterior Glissonean pedicle isolation. 

  Supplementary file 5.  Robotic extrahepatic Glissonean pedicle 4a, 4b, and 4c isolation.
